Ursula's lore -

Ursula is a young 19 year old woman individual who is an extremist with her feminist ideals. She was born and raised in Jäävuoren Korkeudet (Iceberg heights)—a small town in Finland.

She lived alone with her mother Kukka in Jäävuoren Korkeudet—never having seen her father as a child because of his absence. She and her mother struggled dearly to stay afloat and keep food on the table and a roof over their head, but they managed to find a way, and the Ursula managed to get a scholarship to Happy Walrus Kandidaatin Yliopisto when she graduated from high school due to her hard work and 3.9 GPA—a stark contrast in performance in comparison to her classmates who were less enthusiastic about school. Kukka gave birth to Ursula when she was 21.

Ursula moved to Viivin Asunto when she was 18—a big city for education in Finland—to then attend Happy Walrus Kandidaatin Yliopisto (Happy Walrus Bachelor's university) in pursuit of a Bachelor's degree in railway engineering. She decided to pursue railway engineering because of the stories Kukka told Ursula about her always laughing and marveling at the trains that would pass by their disheveled home.

During her time at Happy Walrus Kandidaatin Yliopisto, she was bullied fraternity brothers, and college slackers and other immature men of the like for her flat chest: they would call her names like Päärynä Pohja (Pear Bottom), Luudan Runko (Broom Body), or Rumpupuikon Jalat (Drumstick Legs) over the span of the 7 months she attended the college before dropping out due to harassment.

Before Kukka's was fired from her job for taking off work from her broken left arm 3 months after Ursula dropped out, Kukka worked for Pähkinänruskeat Liinavaatteet (Hazel Linens) as a seamstress for 13 years before being fired. Kukka's mother was 41 at the time of being fired.

After dropping out, Ursula would then form the extremist feminist movement AMAT (All Men Are Trash) and protest against heterosexual relationships between men and women, feed different lies and narratives that men are cruel by nature, and harass different males online in male-focused spaces. Ursula did this out of the pain that internalized in her spirit from the bullying she underwent in college—taking
